_meta.author: scorecard-refine-local autopilot
_meta.note: LOCAL batch: big-city Soros-network DAs (bragg/Manhattan, krasner/Philadelphia, mulroy/Shelby Co TN) + Austin TX council (vela) + status-fix for jesse-watts (NV-02 federal candidate caught by local sheriff-regex selector — suspended campaign 2026-05-29, lost the 2026-06-09 primary; scored at his actual federal tier). Evidence-only; every TRUE/FALSE cited; consistent with fxbg-2026-06 and natl-local-nc precedents (tax-increase advocacy = econ q2 FALSE; pro-abortion pledge/endorsement = sanctity FALSE; public_justice q0 high-bar rule; refuse_state_overreach left null where local-control fights run in the progressive direction against the rubric's intent).

profile.education: AB Harvard College; JD Harvard Law School
profile.background: Former federal prosecutor (AUSA, SDNY) and Chief Deputy NY Attorney General; elected Manhattan DA 2021 (first Black Manhattan DA); re-elected Nov 2025 with 73.8% over Maud Maron (R); term to Jan 2030. Lifelong member and Sunday-school teacher at Abyssinian Baptist Church.

alvin-bragg.notes_set: Manhattan DA, re-elected Nov 4 2025 (73.8% vs Maud Maron R 20.8%); term ends Jan 1 2030. Evidence-finalized 2026-06-10. HARD contrary evidence: signed the June 2022 Fair & Just Prosecution post-Dobbs pledge to refrain from prosecuting abortion; Jan 2022 'Day One' memo downgraded charges, declined prosecution of several offense categories, and limited bail requests to a short list (partially walked back Feb 2022 under NYPD/public pressure); publicly defended NY bail reform; office published transgender/nonbinary-community prosecution recommendations and runs LGBTQ-focused hate-crimes Pride outreach; office policy seeks dispositions avoiding immigration consequences, wants ICE out of courthouses, said at a 2025 debate he is examining prosecuting ICE agents; post-Bruen statement committed to enforcing NY's 'toughest gun laws in the country.' Public-health gun-violence framing (first-ever Executive ADA for Gun Violence Prevention, buybacks, root-cause programs). public_justice q0 null under the high-bar rule: mixed record (daily gun-case partnership with NYPD touted, but the Day One memo declined standalone resisting-arrest charges and drew sustained police-union opposition; no affirmative anti-defund advocacy found). Credit where earned: documented personal public profession of Christian faith in office (christian_liberty q0 TRUE). Soros funding (precise): Soros gave $1M to Color of Change PAC on 2021-05-14, six days after it endorsed Bragg pledging ~$1M; the PAC ultimately spent just over $500K supporting him; no direct Soros contribution (Jonathan Soros + wife gave $20K directly). See data/soros_adjustments.json for the adjustment layer. Daniel Penny prosecution (manslaughter indictment; charge dropped after jury deadlock, acquitted on remainder, Dec 2024) noted for context.

profile.education: BA University of Chicago; JD Stanford Law School
profile.background: Career criminal-defense/civil-rights attorney (~75 civil-rights suits against Philadelphia police before election); elected DA 2017, re-elected 2021 and Nov 2025 (third term, ~76%-24% over Patrick Dugan). PA House impeached him Nov 2022; articles ruled null and void by the PA Supreme Court (Sept 2024) — never tried or removed.

larry-krasner.notes_set: Philadelphia DA, won a third term Nov 4 2025 (~76% vs Patrick Dugan R). Evidence-finalized 2026-06-10. HARD contrary evidence across the board: signed FJP post-Dobbs non-prosecution pledge and called on all 66 other PA county DAs to protect 'reproductive rights'; office LGBTQ+ Advisory Committee (announced for International Transgender Day of Visibility), LGBTQ+ liaison and crime tracker; own Feb 2018 policy memo ended cash bail for ~25 offense categories, set decline-to-charge lists, required prosecutors to justify incarceration cost on the record, and directed below-guideline pleas ('end mass incarceration'); career of ~75 civil-rights suits against Philadelphia PD and prosecution of officer Ryan Pownall; gun violence framed as 'public health and safety crises'; vowed to keep Philadelphia a sanctuary city, called the 2025 federal immigration crackdown 'Nazi stuff' and warned he will charge ICE agents who violate state law; post-Bruen warned of 'open hunting season on all gun regulations' and called PA gun laws 'weak'; publicly campaigned for PA red-flag (ERPO) bills (July 2022). refuse_state_overreach left null: his local-control fights (impeachment defense, Act 40 special-prosecutor suit — lost 4-3 in Commonwealth Court) defend progressive-prosecution discretion, not the rubric's subsidiarity intent. Soros funding (precise): ~$1.7M into the 2017 race via the PA Justice & Public Safety PAC ($1.45M Apr 2017 + ~$214K May 2017). See data/soros_adjustments.json for the adjustment layer. No public profession of personal faith found (mother was an evangelical Christian minister — biographical only).

set.office: District Attorney General, 30th Judicial District (Shelby County)

profile.education: BA Cornell University; JD William & Mary
profile.background: Former DOJ Civil Rights Division attorney (Voting Section); University of Memphis law professor (election law, criminal law); Shelby County Commissioner 2006-2014; elected DA 2022 (56% over incumbent Amy Weirich); 8-year term to Sept 2030. Raised Catholic (parochial schooling) — no public profession of personal faith in office found.

steve-mulroy.notes_set: Shelby County (Memphis) DA, term to Sept 1 2030. Evidence-finalized 2026-06-10. Status verified: all five Taylor-filed ethics/ouster complaints dismissed by the state (Sept 2025); the legislature then enacted HB 483 (AG audit + DA pro tem mechanism, April 2026) and the Memphis Safe Task Force Accountability Act (signed May 7 2026); Mulroy sued the state May 26-27 2026 to block both — litigation pending. Genuine credit where earned: decade-plus documented advocacy for hand-marked paper ballots in Shelby County, continued as DA (election_integrity q0 TRUE, HARD). HARD contrary evidence: abortion prosecutions under TN's trigger ban an 'extremely low priority' and criminal justice 'not the appropriate forum' for reproductive matters; celebrated losing the drag-ban suit (Friends of George's v. Mulroy — 'probably never been happier to lose a lawsuit'); marched in the 2024 Memphis Pride parade and the DA office is a registered Pride parade unit; Standing Bail Order (2023, with Just City/ACLU) overridden by state law; juvenile transfer to adult court 'last resort'; opposes the death penalty and fought the 2023 AG capital post-conviction law (lost on appeal Oct 2024); called on the legislature to repeal permitless carry and adopt extreme-risk (red-flag) orders (June 2023); criticized the 2025-26 ICE surge ('arresting law-abiding, tax-paying people… not making Memphis safer'). public_justice q0 null under the high-bar rule: denies wanting to defund ('not a single one of those things was true'), partners with MPD, touts a 26% homicide drop — denial and routine partnership, not affirmative advocacy; charged all five officers in the Tyre Nichols case (three acquitted of state charges May 2025). Soros funding (precise): Soros gave $280K to the independent People for Fairness and Justice PAC, which spent ~$60K on ads supporting him; Mulroy denies receiving Soros money directly. Listed on the LELDF policedefense.org map. See data/soros_adjustments.json for the adjustment layer. refuse_state_overreach left null: his aggressive local-control resistance (May 2026 suit, capital-case fight, non-enforcement stances) runs in the progressive direction against the rubric's intent.

set.office: City Council Member, District 4 (Mayor Pro Tem 2026)

profile.education: BA (history), MPA, JD — University of Texas at Austin
profile.background: Immigration/criminal-defense attorney (represented hundreds in deportation/asylum proceedings); former board chair, Workers Defense Project; won the Jan 2022 D4 special election, re-elected Nov 2024 (58.5%); serving as Mayor Pro Tem in 2026; term ends Jan 2029.

jose-chito-vela.notes_set: Austin City Council D4 (nonpartisan office; Democrat), Mayor Pro Tem 2026; re-elected Nov 2024 with 58.5%, term to Jan 2029. Evidence-finalized 2026-06-10. HARD contrary evidence: authored the GRACE Act (July 2022, passed unanimously) making abortion-related enforcement APD's lowest priority and called the state ban 'draconian'; introduced the May 2024 resolution (passed 10-1) deprioritizing SB 14 enforcement (gender-transition treatments for minors) — first Texas city — saying 'Trans people deserve the right to self determination'; presented the 'Y'all Means All Day' LGBT proclamation; part of the Aug 2025 subquorum proposing a 6.75-cent property-tax increase and joined the 5-cent tax-rate-election motion (voters rejected it Nov 2025; still favors taxing to the 3.5% state cap); champion of marijuana-enforcement deprioritization and praised Travis County personal-bond magistration ('probably the best magistration system in the state'); led the Jan 2026 APD policy restricting ICE cooperation ('communication with ICE should be an exceptional circumstance') — now under a Paxton investigation. Credit where earned (public_justice q0 TRUE): voted YES on the 5-year $218M police contract (Oct 2024, 10-1), publicly calling it a 'major, major win' that 'accomplishes everything the APOA requires,' and publicly praised police/EMS/fire response to the 2026 6th Street mass shooting — affirmative public backing, no defund support documented (joined council after the 2020 budget cut); also advocates police-oversight enforcement and delayed transfer of 37 civilian positions to APD (2023). refuse_state_overreach left null: his repeated resistance to TX state law (abortion, SB 14, marijuana, SB4/ICE) runs in the progressive direction against the rubric's intent. No public religious profession found.

set.office: U.S. Representative NV-02 (2026 R primary — suspended 2026-05-29, lost primary 2026-06-09)

profile.background: Sheriff-Coroner of Eureka County NV 2019-2024 (elected 2018, re-elected 2022; resigned Nov 1 2024 amid a recall effort he calls retirement; earlier 2021 recall over his 2A stance failed for signatures); Executive Director of The Wounded Blue (injured-officer nonprofit); first GOP entrant in the 2026 NV-02 open-seat race (Amodei retiring).

jesse-watts.notes_set: STATUS FIX (candidacy currency): Watts suspended his NV-02 campaign on 2026-05-29 and endorsed rival James Settelmeyer ('I am announcing the suspension of my campaign for Congress… I am endorsing James Settelmeyer'); his name remained on the 2026-06-09 primary ballot and he took 1.7% (1,079 votes, 8th of 13). David Flippo (Trump-endorsed) won the primary 44.6% over Settelmeyer 35.9%. Marked lost — not a live ballot option. Evidence-finalized 2026-06-10 at the federal tier from his own documented record: as Eureka County sheriff he refused to enforce Nevada's red-flag law (AB 291) and universal background-check law, backed litigation against the red-flag law, and wrote Gov. Sisolak he would resist 'until there is no fight left in me… against you and unconstitutional government overreach' (self_defense q1 TRUE, HARD); May 2020 official statement refused to enforce COVID orders against church services — 'Freedom to Assemble, and the Freedom of Religion. No emergency declaration… can take those god given rights away' (christian_liberty q0 TRUE, HARD); This Is Reno questionnaire: supports voter ID, accurate rolls, secure ballots (election_integrity q1 TRUE); supports codifying Trump's border-closure/enforcement EO in law; opposed Ukraine-war funding and the ACA; deregulation-first economics, open to Freedom Caucus membership; reported as aligned with the Constitutional Sheriffs association (SOFT). Campaign site had no issues page; no findable positions on abortion, marriage/gender, or family policy — left null, not inferred from party.
